content.full_text:
State of California

M e m o r a n d u m

To      :  File                                    Date  : April 25, 1989


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         John McLean

Subject :  Advice No. T-89-094


         Because of the need for an immediate response, this letter
was handled by telephone advice.  The letter was discussed in a
legal division meeting and Assemblyman Eaves was advised that the
mailing did not violate the mass mailing prohibition.  Written
confirmation was not requested.

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
State Capitol
Sacramento
California 95814
(916) 445-4843

COMMITTEE ON AVIATION

JERRY EAVES
ASSISTANT MAJORITY FLOOR LEADER
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

REPRESENTING THE CITIES AND COMMUNITIES OF RIALTO,
SAN BERNARDINO, FONTANA, COLTON, ONTARIO, BLOOMINGTON, AND LAKE ARROWHEAD

February 8, 1989

John H. Larson, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Larson:

This letter is a request for an opinion letter as to the
compliance of the attached postcard to the new regulations under
Proposition 73. The postcard will be sent to residents of the
66th Assembly District, to invite them to Government Day.

My office is coordinating the Second Annual Government Day in
San Bernardino County. The committee is co-chaired by the County
Superintendent of Schools and every other local school
superintendent in the 66th Assembly District. Also, the cities
of Fontana, Colton, San Bernardino, Rialto, Ontario, and Highland
are participating.

Government Day is not a political event, it is a civic event.
Confirmations have been received from over 65 government offices
which will be represented at Government Day. The continued
success of this event is dependent upon participation from the
general public. This is the reason I am planning to send the
attached postcard.

Additional information about Government Day is enclosed for
your review. Please give my Capitol office a reply by
February 14.

Sincerely,

JERRY EAVES

JE:rgs

Assemblyman Jerry Eaves
224 N. Riverside Ave., Suite A
Rialto, California 92376

Bulk Rate
U.S. Postage
PAID
State of
California
Assembly

Government Comes to the People
at the Second Annual

Government Day

More than 70 government offices will be available to answer
your questions on issues such as Proposition 103 (the insurance
initiative), government jobs and programs, and much more.

Learn what government can do for you!

Saturday, March 4th, 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
Central City Mall in San Bernardino

For you,

Government Day!

FEB 09 '89 16:31 ERVES                                                P.1




FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE                          CONTACT: Rebecca Gil
December 28, 1988                                         (714) 820-1902


                                  #61

                    ASSEMBLYMAN JERRY EAVES
                   BRINGS GOVERNMENT TO THE PEOPLE


You can learn to cut through the bureaucratic red-tape in a snip at
Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' Second Annual Government on Saturday, March 4,
1989 from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. at Central City Mall on Second Street,
between 6 and F Streets in San Bernardino. "This event is the equivalent
of Sacramento coming to San Bernardino for a day," said Assemblyman
Jerry Eaves.

It will be a marketplace brimming with government information.
"Shoppers" can get updates on just about every benefit program for
children and seniors and on controversial issues such as the insurance
initiative Proposition 103. A new twist at Assemblyman Jerry Eaves
Second Annual Government Day is that agencies will be recruiting new
employees. So you can come to Government Day prepared to complain or to
court the Government.

Scores of government staffers are giving up their Saturdays "to meet
with the people" in a fabric atmosphere throughout the mall. Enthused
by the success of the 1st Annual Government Day, this year's
participation is expected to exceed that of the over 70 government
offices which took part last year. Every level of government- federal,
state, county and local will be represented. Having been endorsed by the
County Superintendent of Schools and every local school superintendent,
the educational value of Government Day is incredible.

Another interesting aspect of Government Day is the interaction which
will take place between the government staffers. "If you want to know
what happens when the Internal Revenue Service talks to the State
Franchise Tax Board, or when the Department of Motor Vehicles talks with
the Department of Insurance -- come to Government Day and find out. It
promises to be a great day!" said Assemblyman Jerry Eaves.

FEB 08 '89 16:24 EAVES                                P.4


SECOND ANNUAL GOVERNMENT DAY

(714) 387-4386

The Sun

Opinion

Editorials

BROOKS JOHNSON / Publisher
ROBERT W. RITTER / Executive Editor
MIKE WHITEHEAD / Managing Editor
MARIE SAULSBURY / Managing Editor/Sunday
RICHARD B. KIMBALL / Editorial Page Editor

Have some more
'Government Days'

Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' Government
Day concept has proved successful.
His idea was to assemble as many gov-
ernment agencies as possible in one place at
one time, to provide a sort of supermarket
for information for citizens.
This he did last weekend at San Bernardo's Central City Mall. Seventy-seven
agencies, offering information on everything
from university courses to law enforcement,
set up booths for the event.
The event went so well, Eaves, D-Rialto,
said, "I'm going to recommend every assem-
blyman in Sacramento do the same thing in
his or her district at least once a year."
Eaves colleagues should follow his ad-
vice. Government Day is a helpful way to
bring agencies closer to the people they
serve.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 14, 1989


Honorable Jerry Eaves
Assemblymember, Sixty-Sixth District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Letter No. 89-094

Dear Assemblymember Eaves:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on February 8, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about the advice we give,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a mass mailing regarding Assemblyman Eaves' Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing prohibition.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised by telephone that the mailing did not violate the mass mailing prohibition under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional formal advice letter structure with verbatim QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. Instead contains a memorandum stating telephone advice was given, confirming compliance with mass mailing prohibition. Actual question inferred from context of mailing materials and Government Day event documentation included in file. No formal written confirmation was prepared as noted in memorandum.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a mass mailing regarding Assemblyman Eaves' Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing prohibition.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised by telephone that the mailing did not violate the mass mailing prohibition under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter 89-094 regarding Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' inquiry about whether a mass mailing for his Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing rules. The FPPC provided telephone advice confirming the mailing was compliant.
